objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 LTI integration assessment with a leaked shared secret and forged instructor launch.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
LTI consumers, users, course IDs, signatures, and gradebook effects are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map exposed LTI configuration and launch metadata.
actions: Load the synthetic LTI configuration endpoint
Identify whether the consumer key, launch URL, and shared secret are exposed to unauthenticated users
successCriteria: The leaked LTI shared-secret configuration is recorded with a durable evidence ID
noSubagents: credential-handling and disclosure decisions stay with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate forged instructor launch impact from the exposed shared secret.
actions: Create a synthetic signed LTI launch using the exposed shared secret
Submit the forged instructor launch to the lab target
Record the role escalation and gradebook-write authorization impact
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both the leaked shared secret and forged instructor launch evidence
noSubagents: final education-data impact and remediation wording stay with the primary operator

successCriteria: Final handoff lint and operation audit pass

evidence.title: Synthetic public LTI configuration response
evidence.summary: The LTI configuration endpoint disclosed the consumer key, launch URL, and shared secret without authentication.

evidence.title: Synthetic forged instructor LTI launch
evidence.summary: A launch signed with the exposed shared secret created an instructor context with gradebook-write capability.

findings.title: Public LTI shared secret enables forged instructor launches
findings.description: The synthetic application exposes an LTI shared secret through a public configuration endpoint and accepts forged launches signed with that secret.
findings.impact: An attacker could impersonate an instructor, enter a trusted course context, and reach gradebook-write workflows without a legitimate LMS session.
findings.remediation: Remove shared secrets from public configuration, rotate exposed LTI credentials, require server-side secret storage, validate launches against the trusted LMS issuer, and alert on unexpected instructor launches or gradebook-write scopes.
